source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the physical architecture of a welded spin-formed cryogenic LOX tank: cylindrical barrel + forward hemispherical dome + aft hemispherical dome + two welded-boss fittings (fill/vent on forward dome, feed on aft dome) + internal anti-slosh baffles + external mounting saddles for the mid-body stringer-lug BC mentioned in the original prompt + pressure-transducer bosses for instrumented skin-T monitoring. The original prompt permits full-penetration welds, which motivates a realistic weldment decomposition. All part-level counts and dimensions beyond those explicitly anchored to the original sample are marked speculation:true.

full_prompt:
Design a monolithic cryogenic LOX (liquid oxygen) propellant tank for a student-developed LOX/LCH4 bipropellant rocket with total-impulse budget under 200,000 lb-sec. The tank is a seamless 316L stainless steel pressure vessel, spin-formed hemispherical domes, internal diameter 200 mm, cylindrical barrel length 600 mm, one forward fill/vent AN-8 port on the forward dome and one aft feed AN-12 port on the aft dome, both welded bosses integral to domes (welds permitted for cryogenic tanks but must be X-ray inspected). Propellant: LOX at 90 K (boiling). Maximum Operating Pressure (MOP) = 30 bar (3 MPa). Material 316L stainless steel at 90 K (cryogenic derating): Young's modulus 210 GPa, yield strength 450 MPa at 90 K (austenitic steel strengthens at cryogenic temperature), ultimate tensile strength 720 MPa at 90 K, density 8000 kg/m^3. Requirements: (R1) proof pressure 1.5 x MOP = 45 bar held for 5 minutes; max von Mises at proof <= yield / 1.1 = 409 MPa; (R2) burst pressure 2.5 x MOP = 75 bar; max von Mises at burst <= ultimate / 1.25 = 576 MPa; (R3) all welds shall be full-penetration and inspected to ASME BPVC Sec IX; fatigue life for the mission (10 pressurization cycles) shall satisfy stress range margin >= 2.0 (stress range <= fatigue endurance / 2.0); (R4) dry mass <= 6.5 kg; (R5) during LOX loading the external tank skin shall not exceed 100 C nor drop below -200 C on instrumented points; (R6) thermal contraction from 293 K to 90 K shall not produce max von Mises exceeding 250 MPa at mounting lugs. Primary verification: structural FEA at proof (45 bar at 90 K), burst (75 bar at 90 K), plus a coupled thermal-structural step cooling from 293 K to 90 K; vibration analysis NOT required at this gate (first mission gate is pressure qualification); fluid and radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242, weld map, and a report listing wall thickness, dry mass, max von Mises at proof and burst, thermal-contraction lug stress, and mass in kg.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The deliverable is reconfigured as a welded tank assembly. Parts: spin-formed cylindrical barrel shell, forward hemispherical dome, aft hemispherical dome, forward fill/vent AN-8 welded boss fitting, aft feed AN-12 welded boss fitting, two internal anti-slosh baffles, two external mid-body mounting saddles (providing the stringer-lug BC), and two pressure-transducer bosses for instrumented skin-T / skin-stress sensors required by R5. All pressure-boundary welds are full-penetration and X-ray inspected per ASME BPVC Sec IX (R3 preserved). FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED tank only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Weld-zone DCR and flange/saddle bolt/weld DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load cases (LC1 proof, LC2 burst, LC3 cryogenic contraction). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic cryogenic LOX (liquid oxygen) propellant tank for a student-developed LOX/LCH4 bipropellant rocket with total-impulse budget under 200,000 lb-sec.

The tank is a seamless 316L stainless steel pressure vessel, spin-formed hemispherical domes, internal diameter 200 mm, cylindrical barrel length 600 mm, one forward fill/vent AN-8 port on the forward dome and one aft feed AN-12 port on the aft dome, both welded bosses integral to domes.

Propellant: LOX at 90 K (boiling). MOP = 30 bar (3 MPa). Material 316L stainless steel at 90 K: Young's modulus 210 GPa, yield strength 450 MPa at 90 K, ultimate tensile strength 720 MPa at 90 K, density 8000 kg/m^3.

Load cases: LC1 proof pressure 1.5 x MOP = 45 bar held for 5 minutes at 90 K; LC2 burst pressure 2.5 x MOP = 75 bar at 90 K; LC3 cryogenic contraction from 293 K to 90 K; LC4 external skin within [-200, 100] C.

prompt.title: LOX cryogenic propellant tank assembly (student bipropellant rocket) (multi-part)
prompt.brief: 316L stainless LOX tank delivered as a welded assembly: barrel + 2 domes + 2 AN-port bosses + 2 anti-slosh baffles + 2 mounting saddles + 2 instrumentation bosses. MOP 30 bar, 90 K service. Proof 1.5x, burst 2.5x, coupled cryogenic thermal-structural contraction. FEM verification at assembled level only; weld-zone and bolt-zone DCR <= 1.0.

geometric_constraints.welds: full-penetration, X-ray inspected per ASME BPVC Sec IX (allowed for cryogenic tanks)

load_cases.name: thermal_skin_limits
load_cases.description: loading transient; external skin within [-200, 100] C

boundary_conditions.mounting: rear feed boss + mid-body stringer lugs fixed (the two external saddles)
boundary_conditions.internal_pressure: on inner wall of barrel, both domes, and both welded bosses
boundary_conditions.thermal: inner wall held at 90 K; outer wall free convection with insulation layer

parts.name: cylindrical_barrel_shell
parts.role: primary cylindrical pressure-containing mid-section between the two domes; hosts the mid-body mounting saddles and pressure-transducer bosses

geometric_constraints.wall_thickness_mm: sized by hoop stress at LC2 (burst 75 bar)
geometric_constraints.surface_finish: X-ray inspectable circumferential weld lands at both ends

parts.name: forward_hemispherical_dome
parts.role: spin-formed hemispherical forward closure; hosts the AN-8 fill/vent boss; fully pressurized on inner surface

parts.name: aft_hemispherical_dome
parts.role: spin-formed hemispherical aft closure; hosts the AN-12 feed boss; fully pressurized on inner surface

parts.name: AN_port_welded_boss
parts.role: welded boss fitting threaded/flanged to AN-8 (forward, fill/vent) or AN-12 (aft, feed); integral boss geometry welded into the dome; original prompt lists these as welded bosses integral to domes
parts.count_note: Exactly 2: one AN-8 on the forward dome, one AN-12 on the aft dome.

parts.name: internal_anti_slosh_baffle
parts.role: internal ring baffle tack-welded to barrel ID to attenuate axial LOX sloshing during launch; does not carry pressure load but does carry inertial fluid load and drives a fatigue cycle every pressurization

parts.name: external_mounting_saddle
parts.role: external mid-body stringer-lug saddle pair that carries the tank into the rocket airframe; provides the 'mid-body stringer lugs fixed' boundary condition in the original prompt and is the primary location for thermal-contraction lug stress checked in R6
parts.count_note: Exactly 2 (one on each side of the barrel centerline) per typical rocket-tank mounting; teams may specify more.
parts.material_hint: 316L stainless steel or Ti-6Al-4V for thermal-CTE matching

parts.name: pressure_transducer_boss
parts.role: welded threaded boss on the barrel that accepts a pressure transducer and skin-thermocouple harness; required for instrumented verification of R5 external-skin-T limits

geometric_constraints.location: barrel, staggered axially; one per instrumented skin point

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Z-axis along tank centerline, +Z = aft (feed-port direction)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Weld-zone DCR at all four pressure-boundary welds (barrel-forward-dome, barrel-aft-dome, forward-AN8-boss, aft-AN12-boss) shall be <= 1.0 under LC1 proof and LC2 burst.
pass_fail_criteria.metric: max_weld_DCR across IF1, IF2, IF3, IF4

pass_fail_criteria.description: Mounting-saddle bolt DCR (16 x M8 total) shall be <= 1.0 under LC3 cryogenic contraction combined with self-weight.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order shell / solid-shell for pressure wall, solid for welded bosses and saddles
analysis_types: linear static (proof, burst) on assembled model

interfaces.mediated_by: full-penetration circumferential weld, X-ray inspected per ASME BPVC Sec IX
interfaces.load_path: hoop + axial pressure load continuity between barrel and forward dome

interfaces.mediated_by: full-penetration circumferential weld, X-ray inspected per ASME BPVC Sec IX
interfaces.load_path: hoop + axial pressure load continuity between barrel and aft dome

interfaces.mediated_by: full-penetration set-on weld on the forward dome apex
interfaces.load_path: local pressure reaction at the AN-8 boss, transmitted into the forward dome

interfaces.mediated_by: full-penetration set-on weld on the aft dome apex
interfaces.load_path: local pressure reaction + engine-feed line thrust at the AN-12 boss
inertial slosh load into barrel ID; fatigue cycle per pressurization

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M8 bolts per saddle clamping around barrel OD (non-weld, non-pressure-boundary)
interfaces.load_path: tank self-weight + thermal-contraction relative displacement transmitted to airframe lugs
local pressure reaction at each instrumented point

fem_check_policy.rationale: A welded tank's pass/fail condition couples the barrel, both domes, and every welded boss simultaneously; per-part stress is reported but the governing check is the assembled pressure response. Weld-zone DCR is captured by R_asm1.

notes.why_multi_part: The original prompt explicitly permits welds for cryogenic tanks and calls out welded bosses integral to the domes. The tank is therefore naturally a welded assembly of a barrel, two spin-formed domes, two AN-port bosses, internal baffles, external saddles, and instrumentation bosses.
notes.speculation_caveats: Baffle count/spacing, saddle bolt pattern, and instrumentation-boss count are speculative; all other dimensions trace to the original sample.
